title: SQL Formatter
description: Free online SQL formatter and beautifier. Formats SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, and more. Uppercases keywords, adds proper indentation, and puts each clause on its own line — all in your browser.
placeholder: SELECT u.id, u.name FROM users u JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id WHERE u.active = 1 AND o.status = 'pending' ORDER BY u.name ASC
empty: Paste SQL above to format it.

howToUse.label: Paste your SQL
howToUse.description: Paste any raw or minified SQL query into the input box. Formatting is applied automatically as you type.

howToUse.label: Adjust options
howToUse.description: Set the indent size (2 or 4 spaces) and choose whether to uppercase SQL keywords like SELECT, FROM, WHERE.

howToUse.label: Copy the result
howToUse.description: Click Copy to copy the formatted SQL. Paste it into your editor, query tool, or documentation.

faq.question: Which SQL dialects are supported?
faq.answer: The formatter handles standard SQL (SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, CREATE, ALTER, DROP) and common extensions used in MySQL, PostgreSQL, SQLite, and SQL Server. Dialect-specific syntax is passed through unchanged.

faq.question: Does it handle subqueries and JOINs?
faq.answer: Yes. JOINs are placed on separate lines and AND/OR conditions are indented. Subqueries inside parentheses are kept inline for readability — the formatter focuses on top-level clause structure.

faq.question: Are string literals and comments preserved?
faq.answer: Yes. Content inside single quotes, double quotes, and backtick identifiers is treated as opaque and is never modified. SQL comments (-- and /* */) are also passed through unchanged.

faq.question: Is this tool free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free. All formatting runs in your browser with no server involved — your SQL is never sent anywhere.
